title: Alternatives in regular expressions should be grouped when used with anchors
description: Regular expressions that use alternatives (|) with anchors (^ or $) should be grouped to improve readability and maintainability.
message: Alternatives found in regular expressions with anchors should be grouped.
recommendation: Group alternatives in regular expressions that use anchors.

noncompliant.code:
import re
pattern1 = r'^(abc|def)$'
pattern2 = r'^(?:abc|def)$'
noncompliant.description: Non-grouped alternatives in regular expressions with anchors.

compliant.code:
import re
pattern1 = r'^((abc)|(def))$
pattern2 = r'^((?:abc)|(?:def))$'
compliant.description: Grouped alternatives in regular expressions with anchors.

why_is_this_an_issue: Grouping alternatives in regular expressions with anchors improves readability and maintainability, making the code easier to understand and modify.
how_to_fix: Enclose alternatives within a group in regular expressions that use anchors.
